Football.title: Premier League star named in England squad with view to going to World Cup but NOT to play
Football.content:
ENGLAND boss Thomas Tuchel has named Brighton goalkeeper Jason Steele in his latest squad with the view to using him at the World Cup this summer.

But, not to play.





The 35-year-old is firmly the number two option at the Amex Stadium behind Dutchman Bart Verbruggen and has only appeared in three Carabao Cup games this season.

However, Tuchel will likely take a fourth goalkeeper to North America this summer to be a ‘training goalkeeper’ for the other three.

Jimmy Floyd-Hasselbaink, who coached for England under Gareth Southgate, revealed last month on Sky Sports that the practice started under their tenure with the Three Lions.

“What Gareth Southgate did very well, he took a fourth goalkeeper with him for training, for penalties, for finishing drills and stuff.



“Also to keep the harmony between those goalkeepers, because goalkeepers are different.

“The fourth one was obviously somebody not of the 26. It was somebody only for training. It was Tom Heaton, it was somebody that was older.”

Tuchel appears to want to keep that idea going and Steele has emerged as a prime candidate for the role.

BEST ONLINE CASINOS – TOP SITES IN THE UK

Steele is one of five goalkeepers in Tuchel’s latest England squad for friendlies Uruguay and Japan at the end of the month.



Jordan Pickford, Dean Henderson, James Trafford and Aaron Ramsdale join the uncapped Steele in the 35-man squad.

There is also a return for Kobbie Mainoo who hasn’t featured for his country since the 2024 European Championship final loss to Spain, which the Manchester United star started.

There is also room for uncapped Everton star James Garner and recalls for names like Lewis Hall, Fikayo Tomori and Harry Maguire.

However, there is no room for Real Madrid‘s Trent Alexander-Arnold who, after a difficult start to life in Spain, has cemented his spot in the team and returned to form as of late.

Football.title: ‘Is it today, though?’ – Fans baffled as Arsenal wish Ethan Nwaneri a happy birthday on the WRONG DAY
Football.content:
EAGLE-EYED fans clocked that something was not quite right about Arsenal’s birthday message to Ethan Nwaneri.

The Gunners paid tribute to their young star on Friday morning with the message “HAPPY BIRTHDAY ETHAN NWANERI” written alongside a picture of the teenager in his Arsenal kit.





They added the caption: “Have a fantastic day, Ethan!” And a birthday cake emoji was thrown in for good measure.

But there is just one problem.

Arsenal appear to have jumped the gun as Nwaneri does not turn 19 until Saturday.

Every major website – including Arsenal’s own – lists Nwaneri’s special day as March 21.

And that fact was not lost on the X users who pointed out the club’s mistake.

One quoted last year’s happy birthday message from March 21 and wrote: “Why is his birthday different this year?”

Another put: “Admin you rush too much. His birthday is on the 21st.”

A third posted: “Is it today, though?”



BEST ONLINE CASINOS – TOP SITES IN THE UK

And a fourth chimed in with: “Tomorrow is Ethan Nwaneri’s birthday!!! @Arsenal delete it.”

Nwaneri remains the youngest Arsenal and Premier League player ever, having made his debut as a 15-year-old in 2022.

He has been sent on loan to Marseille for the second half of this season, although he will have hoped for more than the three starts he has managed so far.

And in his absence, fellow youngster Max Dowman, 16, has stolen the limelight as Arsenal’s leading young prospect.

Football.title: Adidas unveil World Cup 2026 kits including never-seen-before shirt and retro Argentina top as iconic feature returns
Football.content:
THE final may be four months away – but debutants Curacao may have already won the 2026 World Cup.

The Blue Wave topped their CONCACAF qualifying group to book their spot in North America for the very first time.





Football fans around the world would already have been rooting for the Caribbean island – with a population of less than 160,000.

But now they will surely be most people’s adopted second team in Mexico, Canada and the US this summer.

And it is all thanks to their belter of an away kit.

Sportswear giants adidas have dropped the second strips for their federations – and there are some absolute must-haves among them.



Each of the away kits feature the iconic trefoil “leaf” logo – returning after 36 years away from the World Cup stage – rather than the three bar or “mountain” crest.

All the designs, which go on sale at 9am this morning, are aimed to blend the pitch and the streets, as adidas go for fashionable numbers with a nod to each country’s culture.

SunSport runs you through the 13 away kits – and the stories behind them – for countries who have already qualified for the World Cup – plus European play-off hopefuls Wales, Northern Ireland and Italy.

Buy adidas World Cup away kits

ALGERIA

Going in alphabetical order, we kick off with Algeria, who get a green number with darker green sleeves and red and white detailing.



BEST ONLINE CASINOS – TOP SITES IN THE UK

Look closely and you will find the green vertical stripes on the main body of the shirt, which are all to do with the lush grasses found around the oases of the Algerian deserts.

ARGENTINA

The world champions always like to make a statement and Argentina are no different in 2026 with an away kit that has shades – literally – of the much-loved 2006 version.

The black base is covered with light-blue floral swirls referencing climbing plants – and Lionel Messi will be hoping their bid to defend their crown blossoms at just the right time.



BELGIUM

Not one for the shy among us, this one. Belgium’s away kit is light blue and pink and is a tribute to Belgian artist Rene Magritte- you know the one – and the surrealist movement with its layers of lines and shapes.

Belgium’s new crest – featuring the ball and pitch lines – is also marked throughout the shirt.

And there is a brilliant touch in the neck with a twist on Magritte’s famous “this is not a pipe” phrase – but it has been tweaked to “ceci n’est pas un maillot” – “this is not a jersey”.

COLOMBIA

Colombia and boring are two words that just do not go together, are they? And their football kits are no different.



There is still a flash of bright yellow but the majority of the kit is made up of a unique light blue and dark blue.

Why? Well, the darker shade represents the Pacific Ocean and the lighter shades the Caribbean Sea, both of which are on the Colombian coast. Pretty deep, huh?

CURACAO

Here it is. The one you’ve been waiting the entire history of the World Cup for. And well worth the wait, right?

Curacao’s away kit is a gorgeous pastel yellow with flashes of orange, pink, turquoise and blue details.



That is all to celebrate the capital city of Willemstad, specifically the Punda and Otrobanda districts – a World Heritage site no less – and its vibrant buildings and postcard sunsets.

GERMANY

Germany and adidas. Synonymous. And their navy away kit for the 2026 World Cup is actually a mish-mash of various training kits through the years, celebrating 72 years of partnership between the national team and the brand.

There is a nod to the classic blue quarter zips worn in 1954, the retro blue and white training tops of the 60s, 70s and 80s and the aqua training wear of the 90s.



ITALY

OK, the four-time champions may not have qualified yet and need to come through the Uefa play-offs – but surely this kit alone should give them some form of special dispensation?

The thought of this instant classic of a white vintage number – with an intricate pattern inspired by Italian tailors’ suits – going to waste and not being showcased by the likes of Sandro Tonali and Nicolo Barella this summer brings us to tears.

JAPAN

Stop the count. Can we agree Curacao and Japan share the title of best away kit, please? Because Japan’s alternate shirt for the World Cup truly is a thing of beauty.

It has a white base, black accents and then 12 different-coloured vertical stripes – 11 to represent the players on the pitch and the central red one to represent the red sun on the national flag as a deliberate reminder of the heart of the team: the fans. We’re in love.



MEXICO

Another white away kit, but another unique one that will no doubt prove popular with fans in Mexico and around the world.

The ‘Grecas’ pattern is found across traditional architecture and art – and now their away shirt. Spot the abstract stairs, too, as the co-hosts look to climb to new heights this time around in front of their adoring home fans.

NORTHERN IRELAND

Bad news, guys. We can’t have both Italy’s away kit and this one in North America – because the Azzurri face Northern Ireland in their play-off.



Living up to their Green and White Army nickname, this kit features a simple but highly effective diagonal pattern – and the XII is a nod to the unwavering support, Northern Ireland’s ultimate 12th man.

The main colour is described as “cloud white” – but this kit is making everyone’s days sunnier and brighter.

QATAR

After qualifying for a World Cup for the first time, Qatar have more reason to celebrate.

It may not be the No1 pick for general football fans looking to pick up a cult hero of a kit but the geometry and shapes of the grey kit to reflect the sand dunes of the desert – plus Qatar etched in Arabic into the neck – are a nice touch.



SAUDI ARABIA

Saudi Arabia is known for its riches – and this away kit is right on the money.

The patterns on the front refer to the designs on traditional Saudi garments – and the green and gold details sure make this a fashion statement.

SCOTLAND

Scotland have waited 28 years for World Cup football, last featuring at the tournament in 1998 so you just knew shirts would fly off the rails as the Tartan Army cheer on Steve Clarke’s men.



But sales are only going to be bolstered by this bold but brilliant away kit.

We’re not sure if it’s orange, pink, salmon, coral, apricot or flamingo colour but frankly who cares because this super-sleek design is an absolute winner.

Let’s just hope their fans don’t go the same colour in the intense US summer sunshine…

SOUTH AFRICA

South Africa, sport, green and gold. It’s a winning formula. And adidas have delivered with the only polo shirt number in the line-up.



The vertical stripes are adorned with exquisite detail and the gold touches plus the collar… chef’s kiss. Sublime.

SPAIN

Many people are tipping Spain to go all the way and lift the World Cup at the MetLife Stadium on July 19 – and it would be fitting if they did so wearing this number.

The swirling pattern is tribute to drawings and graphics found across classic books and manuscripts, while the off-white colour represents the paper pages.

Finished off with gold and burgundy accents, it would be hard to begrudge this kit proving to be the shirt of champions. Class.



WALES

And last but by no means least, we have Wales. They need to roar their way past Bosnia and Herzegovina then Italy or Northern Ireland in the play-offs.

But many – ourselves included – would love to see this shirt worn across North America this summer.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

The designers describe this number as a “cream base punctuated with abstract graphical interpretations of the Welsh dragon, in muted red tones, detailed across the front of the jersey” – or, in layman’s terms, another banger.

Or should we say Bangor…?

Football.title: Thomas Tuchel explains reason behind monster size of England squad in final friendlies before World Cup
Football.content:
THOMAS TUCHEL has explained his reasoning behind the decision to announce an enlarged 35-man England squad.

The German boss announced his squad for friendlies against Japan and Uruguay on Friday, but fans were shocked to see 35 names rather than the expected 26.



The German quickly explained that England will adopt an unusual approach to their friendlies – the side’s last chance to experiment before the 2026 World Cup.



Tuchel said: “We decided then to divide it into two camps almost.

“We have a camp where we bring players in that we haven’t seen, that haven’t played so much, to open up the competition for tickets to the US.

“And then from Friday and Saturday a new group of players will come into the camps, will get a rest before, and we will go with a new mix of players into the match against Japan.”

Beyond Tuchel’s brave decision to split his squad – the last he will be able to name before the 2026 World Cup – the selections themselves have drawn mixed reactions.



Kobbie Mainoo is among the players ending their England exile in the squad, following his rejuvenation under interim Manchester United boss Michael Carrick.

AC Milan’s Fikayo Tomori has had an even longer wait to be recalled to the squad, last pulling on the Three Lions shirt in 2023.

Marc Guehi unsurprisingly made the squad, but not without controversy, as England’s official squad announcement still listed him as a Crystal Palace player, despite his transfer to Manchester City in the winter window.

SUN VEGAS WELCOME OFFER: GET £50 BONUS WHEN YOU JOIN



Newcastle have a number of inclusions including Anthony Gordon, Tino Livramento, Lewis Hall and Dan Burn, but there is still no room for Harvey Barnes.

The extra room for inclusions has meant some shocking names have made it into the squad.

Brighton’s Jason Steele is the standout dark horse selection.

The 35-year-old stopper is one of five keepers in the squad, despite having not played a single game of Premier League football this season for his side.

James Garner is another surprising, if deserving, inclusion in the squad, following his impressive form for Everton this year.



Dominic Calvert-Lewin is set for his first appearance for his nation since 2021, having rediscovered his form after making a switch to Leeds in the summer.

Football.title: England make embarrassing squad announcement blunder over sure-fire starter ahead of final games before World Cup
Football.content:
SOMEONE at England forgot that Marc Guehi had moved to Manchester City in January after a squad announcement mistake.

Thomas Tuchel’s giant 35-man group for friendlies against Japan and Uruguay was revealed on Friday morning – with Guehi, 25, still listed as a Palace player.





The defender transferred for £20million earlier this year but was given the wrong team here.

Guehi is one of 11 defenders named by Tuchel in his massive squad including the return of Fikayo Tomori and Kobbie Mainoo.

Every other player had the correct club next to their name with Marcus Rashford even having his loan status at Barcelona clarified.

Guehi is one of FIVE Manchester City players, joined by goalkeeper James Trafford, Nico O’Reilly, John Stones, and Phil Foden.

He has made 26 appearances for the Three Lions since debuting in March 2022, less than half a year after Tuchel sold him whilst at Chelsea.

The German boss instead favoured flop Malang Sarr at Stamford Bridge, leading to Palace picking up Guehi for a bargain fee.

He went on to quickly become Eagles captain and played almost 200 times for the Selhurst Park side.



Guehi has slotted in under Pep Guardiola at City, rapidly earning the manager’s trust.

CASINO SPECIAL – BEST CASINO BONUSES FROM £10 DEPOSITS

He was a big figure for England at the 2024 Euros, starting all but one game – only missing that due to suspension.

Leader Guehi is surely a lock to feature at centre-back at the World Cup later this year.

Who his main partner is remains to be decided with Stones and Harry Maguire recent tournament favourites under Gareth Southgate.

Ezri Konsa has also played regularly for England and will get his chance to impress in the final camp of the season.



Tuchel could name a backline of Chelsea Cobham academy graduates by the time the World Cup comes around with Tino Livramento and Lewis Hall in contention.

Levi Colwill – currently recovering from an ACL injury and a serious doubt for the summer – has played for England previously, and Reece James would have been selected had he not suffered from yet another hamstring blow last week.

Explaining the size of his squad, Tuchel said: “We decided to divide it into basically two camps almost, so we bring players in that we haven’t seen who haven’t played so much to open up the picture, and the competition for plane tickets to the US.

“Then from Friday and Saturday, a group of players will come into camp – ten or eleven players who get a rest before and we will then go with a new group and mix of players into the match against Japan.”

England play Uruguay next Friday (March 27), before then facing Japan four days later on Tuesday, March 31.

Football.title: England squad named with two stars landing first call-ups and Trent among omissions in final friendlies before World Cup
Football.content:
THOMAS TUCHEL has named a monster 35-man squad for the forthcoming friendlies with Uruguay and Japan.

There are recalls to the Three Lions squad for Manchester United pair Kobbie Mainoo and Harry Maguire, while Newcastle’s Lewis Hall and Tino Livramento are back.





There is a first senior call-up for Everton midfielder James Garner and Brighton goalkeeper Jason Steele is named.

AC Milan’s Fikayo Tomori returns to the squad while Leeds striker Dominic Calvert-Lewin receives his first call-up in five years after his good recent form.

There is also a spot in the group for Tottenham forward Dominic Solanke.

But it will be a kick in the teeth for Trent Alexander-Arnold as he is a shock omission, particularly after Reece James was ruled out through injury.



Tuchel will effectively split the squad to give some of the big names a rest.

Tuchel said: “We decided to divide it into basically two camps almost, so we bring players in that we haven’t seen who haven’t played so much to open up the picture, and the competition for plane tickets to the US.

“Then from Friday and Saturday, a group of players will come into camp – ten or 11 players who get a rest before and we will then go with a new group and mix of players into the match against Japan.”

England play Uruguay a week today and then Japan four days later.



BEST ONLINE CASINOS – TOP SITES IN THE UK

Maguire and Mainoo have returned to the England squad in their first call-ups under Tuchel.

Neither player has played for the Three Lions since a 2-0 Nations League win over the Republic of Ireland in September 2024 but both have been rewarded for their good form for Manchester United.

Maguire, 33, looked to be in the international wilderness despite being a key player under Gareth Southgate.



But the centre-half is now expected to win a 65th cap while midfielder Mainoo should add to his current tally of ten.

Both players fell out of favour at Old Trafford under previous boss Ruben Amorim but they have returned to prominence under Michael Carrick.

There is no room for club-mate Luke Shaw who had a chance to return due to England’s crisis at left-back.

Newcastle’s Hall has returned to the squad and although he has two caps, has yet to feature for Tuchel.

Calvert-Lewin, 29, has been rewarded for his fine form at Elland Road having scored ten goals this term while Solanke’s return is a surprise.



There is no place for Bournemouth midfielder Alex Scott who was handed his first senior call-up in November for games against Albania and Serbia.

But that has paved the way for Everton star Garner who has five assists and two goals this season, having started every game for the Toffees.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

Tuchel will name his World Cup squad in late May and England then have two more friendlies in Florida in June, against New Zealand and Costa Rica.

They begin that tournament by facing Croatia in Dallas on June 17. The other games are against Ghana in Boston and Panama in New York-New Jersey.

Football.title: Stunning model-turned-journalist kicked randy Brazil star ‘in the balls’ after he tried to kiss her during interview
Football.content:
A MEXICAN model and sports journalist has sparked a guessing game after revealing she gave a randy Brazilian footballer a karate kick in the balls during an interview in his hotel suite.

Ines Sainz Gallo says she lashed out after the star got too close and tried to kiss her after his agent had ushered her camerawoman out of the room.





The married 47-year-old mum-of-four, who has interviewed the likes of Cristiano Ronaldo and Roberto Carlos during her glittering career, refused to name the star she had left with a painful memory of their meeting.

But the stunning blonde gave away several telltale clues about his identity when she appeared on a podcast with Mexican TV and radio host Jorge Van Rankin – including his nationality and the fact he had recently signed for AC Milan.

Ines, who moved from modelling to sports reporting and turned down an offer to pose nude in Playboy in 2010 after receiving a personal apology from the New York Jets owner over alleged sexual harassment by players and coaches, said of the incident: “AC Milan had just signed a top Brazilian player.

“I agreed an interview with him through the club and at the time he was staying at a hotel across from the Milan Central Station while they were house-hunting for him and he says, ‘I can give you the interview at the hotel’.



“That’s common. So I go with my camerawoman, we enter, and they told us the interview would be in the small living room of his suite.”

But the situation suddenly changed when the footballer’s agent got her colleague out of the room on a pretext and the door was closed behind them.

Ines added: “That’s when I decided this was a trap.

“At first I played dumb to see if that worked and that’s when he started getting closer and closer and closer and wanted to hug me and kiss me.



SUN VEGAS WELCOME OFFER: GET £50 BONUS WHEN YOU JOIN

“It was a terrible moment. I practice taekwondo, so I unleashed everything I had and kicked him with all my might in the balls.

“Then I started banging on the door to get out, my camerawoman opened it and we left.”

Giving away more clues about the player’s identity when she was asked by her interviewer if the footballer was handsome, Ines responded: “No, he’s a horrible thing.”



She went on to tell Van Rankin: “I’ll tell you off camera afterwards who it was but obviously it wasn’t Kaka who’s an absolute gentleman.”

Nearly 40 Brazilian players have played for AC Milan including Ronaldinho and Robinho, who is currently serving a nine-year prison sentence in his homeland over an Italian nightclub rape, making the list of footballers Ines could have been referring to pretty extensive.

But some football fans were drawing their own conclusions last night about who it was most likely to have been.

In the comments section under footage of Ines’ shock revelations, one football fan wrote: “Give this a like if you think it was ####.”

Football.title: BREAKING: Thomas Tuchel reveals reason behind brutal Trent Alexander-Arnold England snub
Football.content:
Thomas Tuchel has revealed that Trent Alexander-Arnold hasn't been included in the latest England squad due to a "sporting decision".

Alexander-Arnold, 27, endured a difficult start to life at Real Madrid following his £10million move from Liverpool last summer, but his form has improved in recent weeks. Indeed, he has started in seven of Real's last 10 matches across all competitions and produced an impressive performance in Tuesday's Champions League Round of 16 second leg win against Manchester City.

However, Tuchel has chosen against including Alexander-Arnold in his 35-man England squad for March's international friendlies against Uruguay and Japan.

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

Explaining the decision to leave Alexander-Arnold out, Tuchel said he already has a clear idea about what he can bring to the camp and has therefore picked other players.

In a press conference at Wembley Stadium on Friday morning, Tuchel said: "No. I know that it's a tough decision for Trent, as it is for Ollie Watkins and Luke Shaw. These tough decisions come with the job.

"It's a sporting decision that we stick with Jarell Quansah, Tino Livramento and Djed Spence who can play for us at right-back. It is a decision for Dom Solanke and Dominic Calvert-Lewin, who I don't know as well as Ollie Watkins. I feel I have a very clear picture of what Ollie can give us, so I want to have a look at two other players.

"I know it's a tough one, I know it's a big name. He's a huge talent with a big career, but I feel I know what Trent can give us and decided to stick to the players that we have had in camp."

A shock inclusion to the England camp, meanwhile, was Brighton & Hove Albion goalkeeper Jason Steele, who has been handed a maiden call-up at the age of 35.

"No one is on the plane to America!" said Tuchel on Steele, who hasn't played a single minute in the Premier League this season. "England has a history of taking four goalkeepers to a tournament which absolutely makes sense and we identified Jason to be a very, very good candidate for this role.

"We wanted to trial it and this March camp is the trial."

There will be more to follow on this breaking news story and Mirror Sport will bring you the very latest updates, pictures and video as soon as possible.

Pleasecheck back regularly for updates on this developing story.

Football.title: Liverpool owners FSG abandon multi-club plan in surprise U-turn
Football.content:
Liverpool owners FSG abandon multi-club plan in surprise U-turn

Liverpool’s current owners Fenway Sports Group had been exploring the prospect of purchasing another club and despite talks having taken place, the Reds’ plans have been shelved

View 2 Images Liverpool's owners FSG were previously looking at purchasing another club (Image: Getty Images)

Liverpool owners FSG have abandoned plans to launch a multi-club model despite talks having taken place. FSG chiefs confirmed plans to purchase a second football club when Michael Edwards was tempted back to the club in 2024.

‌



Early discussions were held, with as many as 25 clubs being put forward. French side Bordeaux were among the possibilities, while Spanish duo Malaga and Getafe were both investigated.

‌



It has also been reported that FSG looked into buying a minority stake in Ligue 1 giants Monaco but chose not to pursue a deal. And according to The Athletic, plans for FSG to become a multi-club ownership model have been shelved, with no immediate suggestion that those plans could be revived any time soon.

‌



President Mike Gordon had told staff back in 2024 that the approach had been launched. In an email the staff, he said: “To remain competitive, we must identify every avenue available to us to gain an edge.

FOLLOW OUR LIVERPOOL FB PAGE! Latest Reds news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Mohamed Salah injury update as Arne Slot reveals latest on Liverpool star READ MORE :

Noa Lang shares photo after surgery on injury as Galatasaray threaten legal action READ MORE :

“To this end, Michael will use every tool at his disposal and has already identified the acquisition of another club as one channel that will help fortify our overall operation and drive our competitive ambitions.”

‌



Meanwhile, Edwards said upon his return: “It was vital for me that, if I did return, it had to be with renewed vigour and energy. In practice, this means having fresh challenges and opportunities.

“As such, one of the biggest factors in my decision is the commitment to acquire and oversee an additional club, growing this area of their organisation. I believe that to remain competitive, investment and expansion of the current football portfolio is necessary.”

View 2 Images Michael Edwards returned to Liverpool in 2024 (Image: Getty Images)

‌



FSG have owned Liverpool since 2010 and have overseen arguably the club’s most successful period in their modern history. The Reds have won two Premier League titles, the Champions League, the FA Cup, League Cup and more since 2019.

While plans over multi-club ownership are not progressing, FSG can boast a number of other sports teams in their portfolio including MLB side Boston Red Sox, NASCAR’s RFK Racing, TGL’s Boston Common Golf and NHL side Pittsburgh Penguins.

They are however set to bank around $1.7billion (£1.27bn) from the sale of the Penguins to Chicago-based Hoffmann family, pending approval from NHL Board of Governors.

Football.title: Mohamed Salah injury update as Arne Slot reveals latest on Liverpool star
Football.content:
Liverpool boss Arne Slot has confirmed that Mohamed Salah is ruled out for the weekend's clash with Brighton due to a muscle injury. Salah was substituted with a potential injury concern during the midweek clash with Galatasaray in the Champions League.

Salah got a goal and an assist in the 4-0 victory at Anfield which saw Liverpool claim a 4-1 aggregate victory over the Turkish club to reach the quarter-final of the Champions League, where they will now face Paris Saint-Germain next month.

Liverpool are back in Premier League action this weekend where they will travel to face Brighton in the Saturday lunchtime kick-off, and will do so on the back of a draw with Tottenham Hotspur in their previous top-flight fixture.

Salah was withdrawn in the second half of Liverpool’s win over Galatasaray with a suspected injury and walked straight down the tunnel after his exit rather than join his team-mates on the bench.

Slot has now provided an update on Salah’s fitness ahead of the clash against Brighton this weekend.

“Unusual [he is injured], I think you expect the outcome so he is not available for tomorrow,” Slot told reporters on Friday. “Good thing for Liverpool is we go to international break, bad news for Egypt, he can't go there.

“We hope with what Mo has shown in the past he can recovery faster than other players. He takes such good care of his body, he can be back earlier than others as history has shown. Only two weeks until we go again.”

FOLLOW OUR LIVERPOOL FB PAGE! Latest Reds news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Discussing Salah's situation at Anfield and his performance against Galatasaray, Slot added: "His (Salah's) first half was not too bad, whole team played well. Individuals, it's helpful if the teams play well. They can then shine.

"It says a lot about his mental strength but that comes with age. If you miss chances, older you get, you get better at handling situations like this. He has always done this really well. He has done this so well in his career, to shut it down and create a goal and an assist, it is the experience he has."

Slot later confirmed with regards to Salah's problem: "It's a muscle issue."

Liverpool will go into the game against Brighton in fifth place in the Premier League table and face an uphill task to qualify for the Champions League.

A top-four finish may be needed for the Reds to qualify for next season’s competition, although fifth place could also be enough depending on where England rank in UEFA’s coefficient standings.

Liverpool are two points behind fourth-placed Aston Villa, five points adrift of third-placed Manchester United and just one ahead of Chelsea, who are sixth.

"Injury-wise, he came off not because he [Salah] felt he had scored enough but because he felt something” Slot said on Wednesday after the win over Galatasaray. "So let's see where he is for the weekend and afterwards."

"It says a lot about him that after you miss a penalty just before half time, that can sometimes be hard for an individual or for a team, so compliments to the team for how we came out in the second half.

"Because we have had a lot of setbacks this season, a lot of first halves or second halves where we have created chance after chance after chance without doing justice to how we have performed - so not scoring.

"And then Mo and the team came out in the second half they way they did, so he assisted Hugo, a great assist and then scored a trademark goal that he has scored so many times in this stadium and for this club, cutting inside, finding the corner.

"So that tells you a lot about his mental strength but definitely also of the team, because adversity is something we can definitely talk about in this season."

Football.title: England squad named as Thomas Tuchel hands out recalls ahead of World Cup
Football.content:
England have named an extended 35-man squad for matches with Japan and Uruguay later this month with Leeds frontman Dominic Calvert-Lewin back in the fold following his impressive season.

Thomas Tuchel is running out of matches with the World Cup just around the corner, but the German has put together a stellar qualifying campaign with the ex-Chelsea boss now just fine tuning his squad.

England will host the South American nation at Wembley on March 27 before welcoming Japan to their London home four days later. The former is likely to provide a sterner test with the team's next outings coming in June, just days before their World Cup opener against Croatia.

The Three Lions have won their last six games on the bounce without conceding with Tuchel showing loyalty to the players who have excelled during the qualifying campaign.

Calvert-Lewin, who was once seen as Harry Kane's back-up, has returned to form this year at Elland Road after several injury-hit years. Everton's James Garner is another new face in the squad with his form rewarded.

Manchester United duo Harry Maguire and Kobbie Mainoo are both included. The pair, at different ends of their international career, have benefited from the upturn in form experienced by the Red Devils. Mainoo can make first England appearance since September 2024 whilst Maguire has a wealth of experience having been a favourite during the Gareth Southgate-era.

Lewi Hall's stellar showings with Newcastle, where he has looked like the best English left-back in recent weeks, haven't gone unnoticed by Tuchel, who has included him in the group.

The German has also opted to name five goalkeepers with Manchester City's James Trafford, who has been starved on minutes this season, still included alongside the likes of Jordan Pickford and Dean Henderson.

Dominic Solanke serves as one of three out and out No 9s alongside Kane and Calvert-Lewin. Aston Villa's Ollie Watkins misses out.

In midfield regular faces Elliot Anderson, Morgan Rogers, Declan Rice and Adam Wharton are named alongside Garner. Despite the fanfare surround him becoming the youngest ever scorer in the Premier League, there is no place for Arsenal's teenage sensation Max Dowman.

England squad

Goalkeepers: Dean Henderson (Crystal Palace), Jordan Pickford (Everton), James Trafford (Manchester City), Aaron Ramsdale (Newcastle United), Jason Steele (Brighton & Hove Albion)

Defenders: Dan Burn (Newcastle United), Marc Guéhi (Crystal Palace), Lewis Hall (Newcastle United), Ezri Konsa (Aston Villa), Tino Livramento (Newcastle United), Harry Maguire (Manchester United), Nico O'Reilly (Manchester City), Jarell Quansah (Bayer Leverkusen), Djed Spence (Tottenham Hotspur), John Stones (Manchester City), Fikayo Tomori (AC Milan)

Midfielders: Elliot Anderson (Nottingham Forest), Jude Bellingham (Real Madrid), James Garner (Everton), Jordan Henderson (Brentford), Kobbie Mainoo (Manchester United), Declan Rice (Arsenal), Morgan Rogers (Aston Villa), Adam Wharton (Crystal Palace)

Forwards:Jarrod Bowen (West Ham United), Dominic Calvert-Lewin (Leeds United), Eberechi Eze (Arsenal), Phil Foden (Manchester City), Anthony Gordon (Newcastle United), Harry Kane (Bayern Munich), Noni Madueke (Arsenal), Cole Palmer (Chelsea), Marcus Rashford (Barcelona, loan from Manchester United), Bukayo Saka (Arsenal), Dominic Solanke (Tottenham Hotspur)

There will be more to follow on this breaking news story and Mirror Sport will bring you the very latest updates, pictures and video as soon as possible. Please check back regularly for updates on this developing story.

Football.title: Man Utd boss Michael Carrick handed injury boost for Bournemouth game
Football.content:
Man Utd boss Michael Carrick handed injury boost for Bournemouth game

Manchester United will be aiming to continue their push for a Champions League spot against Bournemouth on Friday evening with boss Michael Carrick having given an injury update

View 2 Images Michael Carrick takes Manchester United to Bournemouth on Friday (Image: Getty Images)

Bournemouth are set to be without Tyler Adams for their clash with Manchester United on Friday. The Cherries host Michael Carrick’s side at the Vitality Stadium on Friday evening, with both clubs still in the race for a European spot.

‌



United sit third after an impressive run of form under Carrick and could cut the gap to neighbours Manchester City to just four points with victory. Bournemouth in the meantime sit 10th but are just four points behind seventh-placed Brentford with eight games of the season left to play.

‌



But Bournemouth boss Andoni Iraola has confirmed that his side will be without the services of USA midfielder Adams. Speaking in his pre-match press conference, the Spanish coach said: "Tyler is not going to be available, as you suppose, because he hasn't been in the squad for the U.S.”

‌



Adams, 27, joined Bournemouth from Leeds in 2023 and he has gone on to make 55 appearances for the club and has scored twice.

FOLLOW OUR MAN UTD FB PAGE! Latest United news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Michael Carrick gives worryingly honest injury update on Man Utd star READ MORE :

Man Utd 'accept' Elliot Anderson price tag as summer transfer plans become clear READ MORE :

And while Adams is set to be out for the Cherries, United are able to call upon one of their own midfielders once more. Mason Mount has not made a Premier League appearance since mid-January.

‌



He returned to the bench against Aston Villa and Carrick has suggested that he could be in line for a similar role vs Bournemouth. He said: “Starting would be too much at this point, from training time and the exposure he’s had.

“It’s brilliant to have him back, he’s a fantastic player and great to have around the group. The break coming up is a good time for him.”

View 2 Images Tyler Adams is set to miss Friday evening's game (Image: Getty Images)

‌



Despite that positive news, there remains a few United stars ruled out including Matthijs de Ligt, Lisandro Martinez and Noussiar Mazraoui.

Carrick added: "It's [a] similar [situation] really [with De Ligt] and frustrating for Matta. He's obviously trying to work to get back but it's just the back issue, really, that's proving difficult. We'll keep working as hard as we can, to get him back as quickly as we can.

"[Lisandro is] closer, a lot closer. He's getting there, so after this one. I think he'll be alright. [Dorgu] is a bit further down the line but [Mazraoui] is just ill. He just wasn't feeling too good today."

Article continues below

Following the clash with Bournemouth, United have a two-week break due to March's international fixtures before a clash with Leeds United at Old Trafford in mid-April.

Football.title: Arsenal strategy was 'not Mikel Arteta's expectation' as Man City forced plan change
Football.content:
Arsenal strategy was 'not Mikel Arteta's expectation' as Man City forced plan change

Arsenal have morphed into title contenders under Mikel Arteta, but the methods used by the Spaniard are not what many expected after his spell at Manchester City

View 2 Images Mikel Arteta has Arsenal top of the Premier League - but his tactics have not proved overly popular (Image: Rob Newell - CameraSport, CameraSport via Getty Images)

Jamie Carragher believes Arsenal's tactics and strategy were 'not the original expectation' when Mikel Arteta took charge with many expecting him to follow in the footsteps of Manchester City.

‌



The Gunners have been undone by the Etihad outfit several times in recent years, but now hold a nine-point lead at the top of the Premier League as they eye a first title since 2004. Their methods have not proved universally popular however.

‌



Arsenal have become masters of the set piece, scoring a huge amount of goals from corners and free-kicks into the box. It has led to accusations that they are a one-dimensional side and Carragher admits "with some justification" they are seen as a going back to "old-fashioned ways".

‌



Arteta's learnings under Guardiola meant that fans and pundits expected Arsenal to adopt a possession-based style, similar to the one that had bought City so much success.

That has not been the case with Carragher claiming Arsenal have tried to emulate City's eye-catching style, but were ultimately not good enough to match them so have had to find other ways to down them.

He wrote in the Telegraph: "This was not the original expectation when Arteta took the reins at the Emirates. The initial anticipation was that he would redesign Arsenal in the image of his mentor, Guardiola, and in his first few years, this seemed to be the case. However, the limitations exposed when Arteta fielded what was his most attractive line-up two years ago explained why he was compelled to change. That team was very good, but City were simply better.

‌



"Certain long-standing criticisms of Arsenal could not be easily shaken off: they were often accused of being more about image than substance; too soft against the most physical teams; lacking the necessary quality against the elite sides in Europe; and, crucially, lacking the cunning and game-management required to succeed when it truly mattered."

View 2 Images Mikel Arteta was unable to play Pep Guardiola at his own game (Image: David Price/Arsenal FC via Getty Images)

Arsenal remain in contention for all four trophies and could yet claim an unprecedented quadruple. The first of those hurdles comes on Sunday when they face City in the final of the Carabao Cup.

‌



The clash between the pair at the Etihad in the Premier League next month could have a huge bearing on where the title ends up. Their only meeting so far this season saw Arsenal secure a point courtesy of a late equaliser at the Emirates.

Carragher believes that, had Arsenal tried to take on Guardiola at his own game they would've been "doomed to fail." A rethink was required with the Gunners and Arteta finding a "darker, more resolute way to take on his former master."

Football.title: Arsenal 'scrambling to meet Max Dowman demands' after first-team breakthrough
Football.content:
Arsenal 'scrambling to meet Max Dowman demands' after first-team breakthrough

Max Dowman has enjoyed a standout breakout season with Arsenal and it appears that fans are jumping on the bandwagon as the wonderkid’s meteoric rise at the Emirates continues

View 2 Images Dowman made history against Everton with his goal (Image: Getty Images)

Arsenal are struggling to keep up with the growing demand for Max Dowman shirts after his historic breakthrough. The Gunners academy graduate has enjoyed an eye-catching ascent into the first team.

‌



The 16-year-old has made seven appearances for Arsenal this season in all competitions and became the youngest-ever goalscorer in Premier League history in the 2-0 win against Everton.

‌



And that has seemingly sparked a rush of fans attempting to get their hands on a bit of Dowman merchandise. A number of fans have attempted to buy shirts with Dowman’s name on the back at the club shop.

‌



Given his age, Arsenal do not have ready-made shirts, with staff having to make up his shirt with spare letters. Dowman is apparently not even an option on the wall of player names inside Arsenal’s ‘Armoury’ store at the Emirates.

FOLLOW OUR ARSENAL FB PAGE! Latest Gunners news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Phil Foden's place under threat from Max Dowman with Arsenal star on verge of history READ MORE :

A club source quoted by The Sun said: “We’ve been blown away by the sudden demand for Max’s shirt but it’s great to see so many fans wanting his name on their shirt.”

‌



Arsenal boss Mikel Arteta was full of praise for Dowman and his family following his record-breaking goal against Everton.

He said: “That's unique. To achieve what he's done in the context of the game which he's done it in as well, I think it makes it even more special.

“I'm really happy for him. I think he deserves it. His family, and all the people at Hale End have raised him in such an incredible way. One thing is to raise a talent, another thing is to raise a person with such a personality and charisma to play a game with that stature in the manner that he's done it.”

‌



View 2 Images Arsenal fans are keen to buy Dowman's shirt (Image: Getty Images)

Despite hailing his young star, the Arsenal boss has appealed for help from the media to ensure the hype around Dowman doesn’t get too intense.

He added: “I need your help with that, guys. Because if we’re saying that [he needs to be protected] and I have 15 questions on Max, it doesn’t really help.

‌



“If we just bring the temperature down and understand what is the best thing for this boy at his age. He’s doing something phenomenal, we all know that.

“Let’s keep it down a little bit and respect his nature to do what he has to do, which I’m sure is going to be something really good.”

Football.title: Micah Richards makes confident Arsenal prediction - 'Absolutely batter them!'
Football.content:
Micah Richards makes confident Arsenal prediction - 'Absolutely batter them!'

Arsenal are on a collision course with Barcelona in the Champions League semi-finals with Micah Richards admitting they could both get hammered if they get their tactics wrong

View 2 Images Arsenal remain the favourites to win the Champions League (Image: Warren Little, Getty Images)

Micah Richards is confident Arsenal "would absolutely batter" Barcelona if they got their tactics right - but could equally be on the receiving end of a hiding if they approach the game wrong.

‌



The Gunners kept alive their Champions League hopes during the week, which could yet form part of an unprecedented quadruple. They beat Bayer Leverkusen and will face Sporting Lisbon in the last eight, whom they will be heavy favourites to beat.

‌



Arsenal's biggest test will come in the semi-finals, where they will face either Barcelona or Atletico Madrid. The Catalans will be favourites to win that tie and showcased their attacking prowess as they smashed Newcastle 7-2 at the Nou Camp.

‌



Barcelona do take risks defensively, which allowed Newcastle to twice equalise, but are irresistible going forward. Richards admits if they get things wrong at the back then the Premier League side will run riot.

Phil Foden's place under threat from Max Dowman with Arsenal star on verge of history READ MORE :

He said on the Rest is Football: "If you’re looking at Arsenal’s potential route to the Champions League final you’d take that all day and then you’re thinking you’ve got that little buffer in the Premier League, I’d be look at the League Cup thinking it’s a trophy but it’s not the biggest trophy. If they were to lose that, I wouldn’t be losing sleep over it because they are in a perfect position now to win two humungous trophies.

"Arsenal would beat Barcelona. If Barcelona play that high line then Arsenal would absolutely batter them. If they take it back five yards, Barcelona could absolutely batter Arsenal.

‌



View 2 Images Barcelona were at their attacking best as they beat Newcastle

"Martinelli, 100 per cent, would absolutely love to play against that without doubt. Even Gyokeres, he’s not the quickest but he’s a strong runner, all his goals before at Sporting, that’s what he used to do, so I think if they play a high line against Arsenal, Arsenal would absolutely batter them."

Barcelona beat Arsenal in their only ever Champions League appearance, that coming back in 2006 at the Stade de France. Since then the Catalans have downed the Gunners in the knockouts on three occasions. Mikel Arteta's side though remain favourites to conquer Europe this term.

Article continues below

Arsena have won nine of their ten games in Europe this term, but could face a Barcelona side desperate go improve on last year's semi-final exit. They need to first get past Atletico, who got the better of Tottenham and have already beaten their La Liga rivals this season.

Football.title: Merson tells Rosenior: Respect the ball? Just don't say it. Stop it!
Football.content:
Paul Merson says Liam Rosenior needs to stop saying things like "respect the ball" and giving players notes when they are 8-2 down - with Chelsea up against it to qualify for the Champions League next season.

Three straight defeats - twice to Paris Saint-Germain either side of a Premier League loss to Newcastle - leaves the Blues with just Champions League qualification and the FA Cup to play for this season.

All the while, Rosenior has been criticised for saying his Chelsea team "respect the ball" amid their huddle over the centre circle - which encircled referee Paul Tierney in bizarre scenes.

The Chelsea boss was also mocked for giving substitute Alejandro Garnacho a piece of paper with tactical instructions - when they were six goals down with a few minutes left in their Champions League defeat to PSG.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Liam Rosenior is asked for his thoughts on the comments Enzo Fernandez made around his future following the defeat to PSG

Read below for the Magic Man's latest Sky Sports column ahead of Chelsea's next Premier League game away at Everton, live on Sky Sports on Saturday...

Respect the ball? Just don't say it

You don't want to change Liam Rosenior, that's the person he is - very honest. But there's probably a couple of things where he probably would come away and go: why did I say that?

Respect the ball? Don't say it. Just don't say it. I didn't like the Alejandro Garnacho incident against PSG. Even he was walking over thinking: what am I doing with this note? Just stop it.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Tim Sherwood slams Chelsea's controversial pre-match huddle against Newcastle as 'embarrassing' and 'absolute nonsense'

That performance against PSG was worrying. Compare it to Newcastle's game against Barcelona - yes they got beaten heavily but for three quarters of that game they were the better team. I didn't like the way Chelsea lost against PSG.

And I think Chelsea are right up against it to qualify for the Champions League this year. They've got terrible fixtures. If Arsenal had Chelsea's run-in, I'd say Manchester City are still in the title race. That's how hard it is.

If you're Chelsea manager, you're going to be judged on the big games. And Liam Rosenior's had six big games - three against Arsenal, two against PSG and one against Newcastle, and lost them all.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Jamie O'Hara believes Chelsea have 'gone backwards' under the ownership of Todd Boehly following their Champions League exit to PSG

Chelsea have to qualify for the Champions League. I heard Rosenior in an interview a few weeks ago saying: wait until the summer. It sounds like we've got someone coming in, but I'm not sure those type of players will want to come in if Chelsea aren't playing Champions League football.

Will Enzo Fernandez stay at Chelsea?

Chelsea were absolutely ripped to shreds and Enzo Fernandez was just being honest. Will he be here? He just doesn't know.

He'll have to stay, they paid £100m for him and they're not getting that £100m back if they sell him this summer, unless he has an unbelievable World Cup.

With Financial Fair Play rules and everything, they can't afford to let him go for a huge loss, unless they sell an academy player for huge money - and they're not selling Reece James who has just signed a new contract. So I can't see Enzo Fernandez going anywhere.

How many players are Chelsea away from challenging?

Chelsea are a million miles away from the Premier League. They're very inconsistent and they've got no real experience - even though they have players who won World Cups.

They haven't got Thiago Silva who for me has been majorly, majorly missed since the day he left. Chelsea keep on buying young players when that experience is needed.

Against PSG, when you go 3-2 down in that first leg, that experience gets the team together and makes sure it stays at 3-2. It may go to 4-2, you've still got a chance. But don't make it bigger then 4-2, and it goes to 5-2. They way they were beaten, I just can't get my head around it.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Gary Cotterill rounds up all the big talking points from a disappointing night at Stamford Bridge that saw Chelsea well beaten by the holders PSG

They absolutely need a new goalkeeper in the summer. I don't know why they didn't go for Gianluigi Donnarumma. Him and David Raya, they save you points. If Raya wasn't at Arsenal, they wouldn't be in the position they are now.

For every great Raya save, I could match that with a mistake from Robert Sanchez or Filip Jorgensen. Those two give away points every now and again. I go to Stamford Bridge to watch the games and they create nervousness to the fans. And if you're giving that to the fans, what are you giving to the players?

Since day dot, go through every league winner and very rarely do you see people win things with a 6/10 or 7/10 goalkeeper.

Football.title: Japanese GP: UK schedule and how to watch on Sky Sports
Football.content:
Round three of the 2026 Formula 1 season takes the sport to one of its most legendary and revered circuits - Suzuka - for the Japanese Grand Prix.

Whatever the regulation era, the skill of a driver and the performance of a car are put to the test around the acrobatic figure-of-eight Suzuka layout which features some of the most famous corner sequences in F1.

After one-two finishes in each of the season's first two grands prix, Mercedes will be aiming for their third successive win with Kimi Antonelli, F1's newest race winner after an accomplished victory in China, looking to see if he can build in-house momentum against championship-leading team-mate George Russell.

Ferrari will again likely be the closest threat to the Silver Arrows as they bid to improve on their three-four finishes in Australia and China in search of their first Grand Prix win since 2024 - and a first at Suzuka since the golden days of Michael Schumacher in 2004.

Max Verstappen is undefeated around Suzuka in both qualifying and the race since F1 returned to Japan after the Covid pandemic in 2022, but the Red Bull driver will have his work cut out to get anywhere near that this time with Red Bull off the pace so far in F1's new season.

The same performance gap applies to world champions McLaren, who will first be keen to just make the race start at Suzuka after their disastrous double DNS in Shanghai.

With the Japanese Grand Prix now serving as the final F1 action for five weeks following the cancellation of the Bahrain and Saudi Arabian rounds in April, make sure you follow all the live action on Sky Sports F1.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Highlights from the 2025 Japanese Grand Prix from the Suzuka Circuit

Ask a Formula 1 driver to name their favourite circuit and there's a good chance their answer will be Suzuka.

The figure-of-eight 3.6-mile track features an array of famous fast corners known by name such as the Esses, the Degners, Spoon and 130R, which all combine to create one of motorsport's most challenging layouts for drivers and their cars.

On the F1 calendar in all but four seasons since its 1987 debut, Honda-owned Suzuka's original position at the tail end of the calendar means it is associated with some of the sport's most famous title-deciding races including Ayrton Senna vs Alain Prost - twice.

Occupying an earlier April slot since 2024, the F1 fraternity now visits during Japan's famous and picturesque cherry blossom season.

Japanese GP weather forecast in Suzuka

Rain is often a factor during the Japanese Grand Prix weekend but there is no wet weather on the current forecast for Suzuka.

It will be cool though, with temperatures in the high teens throughout the three days of action.

Football.title: Trent omitted from shock 35-man England squad as Maguire and Mainoo return
Football.content:
Trent Alexander-Arnold was the big omission from England's shock 35-man squad as Harry Maguire and Kobbie Mainoo were called up for the first time under Thomas Tuchel.

Aston Villa's out-of-form striker Ollie Watkins also misses out despite Tuchel naming 10 forwards in an extraordinary squad selection that hands a group of fringe players one last audition before the 2026 World Cup.

Leeds' Dominic Calvert-Lewin and Tottenham's Dominic Solanke are the two out-and-out strikers called up, along with Harry Kane, to compete for the role of the captain's back-up in friendlies against Uruguay (March 27) and Japan (March 31) at Wembley.

Brighton's Danny Welbeck and Al Ahli's Ivan Toney have been overlooked in a blow to their World Cup hopes.

"I know it's a tough decision for Trent as it is for Ollie Watkins and Luke Shaw," said Tuchel when asked about the omission of such senior players so close to a World Cup squad announcement.

"These tough decisions come with the job. It's a sporting decision. We stick with Quansah, Livramento and Spence.

"It's a decision for Solanke and Calvert-Lewin, who I don't know as well as Watkins. I have a clear picture of what he can give us. I want to have a look at other players to get a clearer picture.

"I know it's a big name, a huge talent and big career. But I know what Trent can give us."

Some of the players not included in the squad... Trent Alexander-Arnold

Ollie Watkins

Luke Shaw

Morgan Gibbs-White

Harvey Barnes

Jarrad Branthwaite

Conor Gallagher

Danny Welbeck

Ivan Toney

Everton midfielder James Garner has earned his first inclusion in a senior international squad, while there is also a shock recall for AC Milan defender Fikayo Tomori, who last played for England in 2023.

Maguire and Mainoo are the Manchester United players Tuchel has called up as England boss, but Luke Shaw has been omitted from this extended squad.

Tuchel says he has chosen this squad, which will essentially be split up into separate camps for each game, "to open up the competition for plane tickets to the US".

Only 24 of Tuchel's squad will join up for the first friendly against Uruguay, before 11 others arrive in camp ahead of the Japan game as the manager looks to balance his needs against a punishing domestic schedule.

Who are the 11 players who will join late? Dean Henderson

Ezri Konsa

Dan Burn

Marc Guehi

Nico O'Reilly

Elliot Anderson

Declan Rice

Morgan Rogers

Harry Kane

Anthony Gordon

Bukayo Saka

"All of these guys have played 3,500 minutes or more this season," explained Tuchel. "Some have already played more minutes than the whole of last season and there is still a lot of football to play.

"All of them have contributed in September, October or November and have a bit of credit with me. We think that to give them a break mentally and physically will benefit us. They will come with hunger."

Brighton's back-up goalkeeper Jason Steele, who is uncapped and has not played in the Premier League since August 2024, is named in the squad, with Tuchel planning a special role for him at the World Cup - if selected.

Revealing England would take four goalkeepers to the tournament, Tuchel said: "[Steele] will support the goalkeeper group. He will support the goalkeeper coach.

"He will support a penalty-taking group and take a lot of workload off the other shoulders. That's why we need an experienced guy."

Alex Scott and Nick Pope were named in the November England squad. They both miss out here, with Pope now having lost his place at club and international level to Aaron Ramsdale.

Ramsdale and Man City's James Trafford are in the squad along with first-choice Jordan Pickford and Dean Henderson.

Trevoh Chalobah and Reece James were in the November squad but are now out injured.

Tuchel explains 'extraordinary' squad selection

Explaining his extraordinary squad selection, Tuchel said: "We decided to divide it into two camps, almost. We have a camp where we bring players in who we haven't seen, who haven't played so much to open up the competition for plane tickets to the US.

"From Friday/Saturday, a group will come into camp who get a rest before. We will go with a new group of players into the match against Japan."

Sky Sports News has been told that each nation will be able to select 26 players for this summer's tournament.

'Never seen a camp like it' - will Tuchel experiment work?

Sky Sports News football correspondent Rob Dorsett:

"The 35 players' selection is fascinating. Not been done before, has it? It's a new approach and the one thing we've seen from Tuchel is that he's not afraid to rip up the copybook and try things.

"It's innovative. It's interesting to see if the dynamic works. If he's that clearly delineated in terms of these are our new guys, let's see how they get on and this is our more established players that we will give more rest to.

"That's going to lead to an interesting dynamic when it comes to picking the squad and seeing who is available and who is still in the camp by the time we get to that first game a week from tomorrow.

"I'm hugely surprised there's no Trent Alexander-Arnold.

"You have to say, he will be very fearful for his chances of making the World Cup squad now."

Full England squad list

Goalkeepers: Dean Henderson, Jordan Pickford, James Trafford, Aaron Ramsdale, Jason Steele.



Defenders: Dan Burn, Marc Guehi, Lewis Hall, Ezri Konsa, Tino Livramento, Harry Maguire, Nico O'Reilly, Jarell Quansah, Djed Spence, John Stones, Fikayo Tomori.



Midfielders: Elliot Anderson, Jude Bellingham, James Garner, Jordan Henderson, Kobbie Mainoo, Declan Rice, Morgan Rogers, Adam Wharton.



Forwards: Jarrod Bowen, Dominic Calvert-Lewin, Eberechi Eze, Phil Foden, Anthony Gordon, Harry Kane, Noni Madueke, Cole Palmer, Marcus Rashford, Bukayo Saka, Dominic Solanke.

Football.title: Littler: I'm chasing Jonny - I want to finish top again
Football.content:
Luke Littler celebrated his second nightly win of the Premier League season in Dublin on Thursday and now the 2024 champion has vowed to chase down league leader Jonny Clayton.

Littler produced a remarkable comeback from 5-0 down to beat Gerwyn Price in a last-leg decider and win night seven of the Premier League in Dublin.

The world No 1 had coasted past Stephen Bunting 6-3 in Thursday's opening match at the 3Arena to record a first win in Dublin.

He then had to dig in to fight back and battle past Michael van Gerwen in a last-leg decider.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Highlights of the final between Luke Littler and Gerwyn Price

In the final, it looked like Price was set for a second successive victory in Dublin after the Welshman roared into a 5-0 lead.

However, after Littler finally got a leg on the board, turning to gesture jokingly in celebration to the crowd, the momentum swung as Price crumbled.

Five match darts came and went as Littler continued to eat into the deficit before setting up a final-leg shootout, which the world champion clinched when taking out 81 on double 15.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Littler responded to the Dublin crowds sarcastic cheers after finding himself 5-0 down to Price

"I played very well against Stephen and got the two points," said Littler, who moves up to second in the table on 16 points, three behind Clayton, with Price sitting third.

"Then it was another brilliant game with Michael. The final? Nothing to say.

"I am just closing that gap on Jonny. Obviously I have finished top the last two years and I want to do it again.

"Jonny and the start he has had, it is good to see I am in second, but I am right behind him now and chasing."

Will he change his shirt for Dublin crowd in the future?

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player There were 170s galore in the semi-final between Littler and Michael van Gerwen

Littler drew the ire of the Dublin crowd, but he was hugely impressed with the atmosphere at the 3 Arena.

The back-to-back world champion, though, won't be changing his shirt to a green one anytime soon.

"It is incredible and as the Premier League goes on you can forget what the crowds are like," he said.

"Obviously Dublin is very loud and not one to come to if you are not wanting to win and the crowd aren't wanting you to win.

"I experienced that tonight, but I am still learning to not give it to the crowd and just do my job.

"Even from the first game they were chanting 'Bunting', but they will boo you then as soon as the song kicks in they will start cheering you on.

"I build on it, don't give the crowd a reaction. It is nothing against Stephen (Bunting), I just wanted to get the job done.

"It is what it is, people want to see new winners, but I have won again.

"I think last year Nathan Aspinall and Rob Cross had the green shirts and stuck with the red for Manchester. It might be a bit different from me to change the shirt. If they like me next year then maybe in 2028 I might change."

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Highlights of the quarter-final between Littler and Stephen Bunting

What's happening on Premier League Night Eight in Berlin?

Night Eight of the BetMGM Premier League will take place in Berlin on Thursday March 26, as Littler begins his bid for a third nightly victory of 2026 against Bunting at the Mercedes-Benz Arena.

League leader Clayton will take on Rock in the evening's opener, Humphries and Van Gerwen also go head-to-head, while Price takes on Van Veen for a place in the semi-finals.

Night Eight - Mercedes-Benz Arena, Berlin Thursday March 26

Quarter-Finals

Jonny Clayton vs Josh Rock Luke Humphries vs Michael van Gerwen Luke Littler vs Stephen Bunting Gerwyn Price vs Gian van Veen

Quarter-Final fixtures for 14 of the 16 league nights are in draw bracket order, with fixtures for Night Eight and Night 16 determined by league standings heading into each night.

Who will win this year's Premier League Darts? Luke Humphries will be defending his title and you can watch the action every single Thursday until May on Sky Sports. Stream darts and more top sport with NOW.

Football.title: Today on Sky Sports Racing: Came From Nowhere chases hat-trick at Newbury
Football.content:
We have a busy day’s racing on Friday with action from Lingfield, Newbury and Wolverhampton, live on Sky Sports Racing...

2.00 Newbury - Came From Nowhere and Le Frimeur clash in cracking opener

A strong opening BetVictor Novices' Hurdle where Came From Nowhere and Le Frimeur feature among a field of eight.

Jeremy Scott's Came From Nowhere has improved with each start over hurdles and having scored at Hereford in December he landed a decent handicap at Ascot the following month. That was a comfortable success, and he must have obvious claims, but has not been seen since and has undergone wind surgery too.

Le Frimeur was the subject of positive reports before making a winning debut at Chepstow in November and given he has been off since, a market watch is advised.

Of the remainder, Itsinthename arrives in form having landed a Catterick maiden on his first start for Nick Scholfield and Doujadou caught the eye when behind Bossman Jack at Chepstow.

7.00 Wolverhampton - City Of Poets and First Principle contest valuable handicap

City Of Poets and First Principle clash in the main event of the evening, the SBK: Dont Settle For Less Handicap at Wolverhampton.

James Tate saddles City Of Poets who was last seen winning over this course and extended mile distance in November and with that form looking strong, he will be fancied to make a winning return and take his all-weather record to three from four.

First Principle is the main danger for William Haggas and Tom Marqaund. Second in the Lincoln Trial here on his return from a break, he will strip fitter and can go close off a 1lb higher mark.

Plenty of alternatives include Advancing, while Dosman and Zryan are closely matched on their Southwell run last month.

2.42 Lingfield - Alba Gu Brath and Felix Gem head field

In-form pair Alba Gu Brath and Felix Gem feature among a field of seven for the competitive Always Gamble Responsibly At BetMGM Handicap at Lingfield.

Alba Gu Brath, runner-up on both previous starts, stepped up on that form to make all at Newcastle in January. Having still showed signs of greenness on that occasion, he could have more to offer and warrants respect as he tackles handicap company for the first time.

Felix Gem justified favouritism when getting his first success in a novice stakes at Chelmsford last month and makes plenty of appeal on this handicap debut stepped up to a mile and a quarter.

Impossible Mission is up 5lb for his Wolverhampton victory, while the well-bred Al Azd is another for the shortlist.

Best of the rest

3.32 Newbury - Peacenik and Botox Has feature. Supreme Malinas will be popular for the Skeltons.

3.00 Newbury - Cornish Storm will be fancied for Stuart Kittow.

2.30 Newbury - Fair Class Three with Lylian and He Knows Better among five runners.

4.07 Newbury - Plenty of last-time winners in here including Kallista Love.

5.16 Newbury - Hunters Chase with Mister Coffey the pick.

5.25 Lingfield - Goldmoyne bids for a four-timer.

3.12 Lingfield - Profit Street seeks a hat-trick.

Football.title: Sungjae Im leads Valspar by one, Schauffele and Fitzpatrick in contention
Football.content:
Sungjae Im returned to form as an opening 64 has him seven under and leading Brandt Snedeker by one after day one of the Valspar Championship.

Sungjae had missed two straight cuts upon his return to golf from a wrist injury, but was back to his best on Thursday as he carded two eagles through his opening round.

Sungjae holed eagle putts of 18 feet on the par-five 11th and 35 feet on the par-five first after making the turn. Only a pair of bogeys over his last four holes slowed him.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player There was a very unusual incident at the Valspar Championship, where playing partners Rasmus Neergaard-Petersen and Isaiah Salinda putted at the same time and their balls collided, leaving commentators baffled.

Snedeker hasn't won on the tour in eight years and had only two top 10s in the last year but, as the USA captain for the Presidents Cup, he made the most of a sponsor exemption to get off to a strong start at the Copperhead course at Innisbrook.

The 45-year-old carded a bogey-free round at Innisbrook for the first time in 14 years, rattling off three straight birdies to start the back nine in his round of 65.

"Had a really good game plan - just relied on my putter, Snedeker said aftewards. "I putted great today, made a bunch of footage of putts, and put myself in position off the tee, hit a lot of fairways and greens.

"Really excited about how it started, but a long way to go from here."

Davis Thompson is alone in third after shooting 66 on day one, with Pierceson Coody, Andrew Putman and Billy Horschel one worse off at four under after opening 67s.

Horschel, who missed The Players Championship last week, is running out of time to either win or get back inside the top 50 to qualify for The Masters next month.

Two-time major champion Xander Schauffele and Jacob Bridgeman, a winner on tour already this year at the Riviera, also enjoyed bogey-free first rounds.

The pair were part of a large group that includes Matt Fitzpatrick - runner-up at The Players - to shoot 68 and sit three under par.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Matt Fitzpatrick expresses his disappointment after missing out on The Players win.

Corey Conners shot 69, an ordinary round except for an extraordinary moment where he made consecutive eagles round the turn, at the 18th and then the first.

Three-time major winner Jordan Spieth also opened with a round of 69, though at one stage it looked as though he was set for a much better score when five under for his front nine.

But things turned sour as he missed a putt from four feet for birdie at 11, another from similar distance for par at the 13th and then dropped three shots over the closing three-hole stretch known as "Snake Pit," including a double bogey on the 16th when driving into the water.

Defending champion Viktor Hovland was two over through six holes before ultimately salvaging a one-under 70, while Brooks Koepka made his return to Innisbrook with level-par 71.

Watch round two of The Valspar Championship live on Sky Sports Golf from 11.30am on Friday. Stream golf contract-free.

Football.title: How Tyne-Wear derby can shape Newcastle's future after Barca thrashing
Football.content:
Eddie Howe reckons Sunderland at home is "a good game" for his side to head into after their hammering at Barcelona. But it's a game he and his side now dare not lose.

A Tyne-Wear derby double defeat to their fierce rivals, on the back of the 7-2 hammering in Spain which ended their Champions League hopes, would have alarm bells ringing loudly at St James' Park.

Sunderland would go above their near neighbours. Howe would have the unwanted record of being the first Magpies boss ever to lose his first two derbies.

But beyond the pain of the hit to local pride, it would also raise major questions about the direction Newcastle are heading in.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Newcastle United manager Eddie Howe was left disappointed following their devastating 7-2 loss at Barcelona.

"This is where we want to be," said Howe after the loss at Barcelona, reflecting on his enjoyment of how his side went toe-to-toe with the Spanish champions in the first half before it all went wrong after the break.

But it could be years before Newcastle are back on the Champions League stage.

Right now, their Premier League position is precarious.

Newcastle could be in the bottom half if they don't win on Sunday. With hopes of repeating last season's cup success already over, a top-five finish may already be out of their grasp.

Newcastle United

Sunderland Sunday 22nd March 11:00am Kick off 12:00pm

The gap of seven points to fifth-placed Liverpool looks incredibly difficult to bridge, especially given Newcastle's inconsistency this season. It's six points to Chelsea in sixth, and three to Brentford in seventh.

Recent wins over Man Utd and Chelsea had brought belief there could be a late charge up the table. There was a feeling going into Wednesday that Newcastle were perhaps in their best moment of the season.

But the psychological damage of that heavy defeat in Barcelona has wrecked that - and put prospects of a surge towards European qualification in doubt.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Sky Sports News' Keith Downie reacts to Newcastle United's 7-2 defeat at Barcelona in the Champions League.

There was a physical impact, too, with Sandro Tonali now facing a late fitness test to be available this weekend after picking up an injury.

That follows noise over the past week about his future, with his agent talking up Tonali's ambition to take the next step in his career. Bruno Guimaraes has also been linked with a move away.

Champions League qualification wasn't enough to convince Alexander Isak to stay. Without a place in Europe's elite club competition - or even the Europa League - Newcastle may find it difficult to hold onto more prized assets this summer. Attracting prime targets would also become harder.

Image: When will Newcastle be on the Champions League stage again?

Has Howe taken Newcastle as far as he can? That will be another question posed if this season finishes on a down note.

The Englishman has written his name in the club's legend, ending the long-awaited trophy wait with the Carabao Cup triumph and twice taking the team into the Champions League. It's significant work, given Newcastle were fearing relegation when he came in, and it has earned Howe many supporters within the club and among the fanbase.

His hunger to refresh and rejuvenate the squad will be a topic, especially given the draining past summer he had when sporting director Paul Mitchell left. Or could a fresh voice get more out of this group?

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Highlights from the Premier League clash between Sunderland and Newcastle.

They are all issues for another day. For now, the immediate focus is Sunday - and a Sunderland side which pose real threat, despite their recent drop-off in form.

If Newcastle defend set-pieces against Regis Le Bris' side like they did against Barcelona, they'll be in big trouble. 'Unforgivable' was how Howe described their defensive lapses in those moments.

Sunderland - who won the reverse fixture at the Stadium of Light in December thanks to a Nick Woltemade own goal - also come into the game with recent history on their side. They have won seven of the last eight of these fixtures.

They will need no motivation to go all guns blazing to extend that record.

An early goal for the visitors would really have Newcastle nerves jangling. And after their collapse at the Spotify Camp Nou, the fear will be another disastrous defeat is coming.

Howe and Newcastle must bounce back. "We've just got to play like our lives depend on it," said the head coach.

The trajectory of the club could be shaped by the outcome.

Football.title: Fernandes top? Sky Sports ranks top 25 Premier League players right now
Football.content:
Wayne Rooney described Manchester United's Bruno Fernandes as the best player in the Premier League this season. Is he right?

We asked our reporters and journalists to rank a longlist of 40 of the best players in the competition right now and took an average of their answers to produce the Sky Sports top 25.

The list includes players from nine different clubs, with leaders Arsenal the best represented, accounting for seven of the top 25. But which player takes top spot? And how do the rankings below compare to our last vote at the start of the season?

25th: Casemiro

Casemiro clinches a place in our top 25 having enjoyed a resurgence at Manchester United this season. His total of seven league goals is the highest of his illustrious career.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Casemiro on the reception he has received from Man Utd fans this season

24th: Enzo Fernandez

Described by Sky Sports pundit Jamie Carragher as one of the best midfielders in the Premier League earlier this season, Chelsea's Enzo Fernandez comes in at 24th.

23rd: Marc Guehi

His outstanding performances for Crystal Palace earned him a January transfer to Manchester City, where he immediately became an important player for Pep Guardiola.

22nd: Bryan Mbeumo

The summer signing from Brentford has looked comfortable in his new surroundings ever since his arrival at Manchester United and ranks as their joint-top Premier League scorer with nine goals.

21st: Bernardo Silva

A player considered by Pep Guardiola to be one of the best he has coached. Bernardo Silva was one of few Manchester City players to deliver consistently last season and continues to be key.

20th: Elliot Anderson

Image: Elliot Anderson has starred for Nottingham Forest this season
